title: Reading and Use of English - Part 2
description: For questions 9 - 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: The Black Death, which swept across Europe in the mid-fourteenth century, remains one of the most devastating pandemics in recorded history, (0) which is believed to have killed between a third and a half of the entire population. The social disruption it caused was profound and long-lasting. (9) Although the disease originated in Central Asia, it spread with terrifying speed along established trade routes, reaching Western Europe by 1347. (10) Such was the scale of mortality in certain regions that entire villages were abandoned and agricultural land fell into neglect. The collapse of the labour supply fundamentally altered economic relationships, and (11) Provided that surviving workers were willing to move from their traditional estates, they could now demand significantly higher wages from landowners. Not (12) only did the Black Death reshape the feudal economy, but it also prompted a profound transformation in attitudes towards death, religion and the fragility of life. (13) Regardless of their social position, no section of medieval society was spared, from the poorest rural labourer to the most senior members of the clergy. Not (14) until the gradual development of quarantine measures in the later fourteenth century did European cities begin to form more systematic responses to epidemic disease. (15) Whereas earlier historians focused primarily on its catastrophic mortality rates, more recent scholarship has explored the social and political changes it accelerated. The period that followed was, paradoxically, one of significant social mobility. (16) Whether judged as a catastrophe or as a catalyst for change, its consequences for European history were transformative and enduring.

example.text: The Black Death, which swept across Europe in the mid-fourteenth century, remains one of the most devastating pandemics in recorded history, (0) which is believed to have killed between a third and a half of the entire population.

questions.explanation: Concessive conjunction used to introduce a contrast or concession between two clauses.
Emphatic determiner in the inverted structure 'Such was/is the [noun] that...' to express a high degree.
Conditional conjunction meaning 'on the condition that', used to introduce a condition and its result.
Part of the correlative conjunction 'Not only... but also', which requires subject-auxiliary inversion after 'Not only'.
Part of the fixed prepositional phrase 'Regardless of', meaning 'without being affected by'.
Part of the 'Not until' negative fronting structure, which requires subject-auxiliary inversion.
Contrastive conjunction meaning 'in contrast to the fact that', used to compare two different scholarly approaches.
Conjunction introducing two alternatives or possibilities in a summarising conclusion.
